commit.message:
feat(host): O(1) entity prefix router with zero-allocation dispatch

EntityPrefixRouter resolves entity names from request path segments
using branch-friendly prefix comparisons over ReadOnlySpan<byte>.

Design:
- Entities assigned contiguous ordinals at startup (EntityRoute struct)
- 26-slot dispatch table indexed by first ASCII character
- Within each bucket, sorted by length descending (longest first)
  to ensure deterministic shared-prefix resolution
- Hot path: 1 byte read + 1 array index + 1-2 SequenceEqual calls
- No hashing, no dictionary lookups, no string allocations

API:
- Build(IReadOnlyList<EntityRoute>) — build from named routes
- Build(IReadOnlyList<string>) — auto-assign ordinals 0..N-1
- TryResolve(ReadOnlySpan<byte>) — resolve UTF-8 entity slug
- TryResolve(ReadOnlySpan<char>) — stack-encode and resolve
- TryParseAndResolve(path, prefix) — extract entity from full path
  e.g. /ui/customer/123 with prefix /ui/ → ordinal 0, remainder 123
- Match(span, literal) — exact byte comparison via SequenceEqual
- MatchIgnoreAsciiCase — ASCII case-insensitive comparison

Metrics (thread-safe Interlocked counters):
- entity_dispatch_time (avg nanoseconds)
- entity_match_attempts (total comparisons)

30 tests covering: build, resolve, case-insensitivity, shared prefixes,
path parsing, metrics, edge cases, rebuild, stress (26K dispatches).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(backend): fix JSON tag mismatch for bounty reserve fields in repoMetaState

The repoMetaState struct used json tags 'bounty_reserve_order_id' and
'bounty_reserve_status', but mergeRepoMeta() stores these values with
'repo_' prefix ('repo_bounty_reserve_order_id', 'repo_bounty_reserve_status').

This mismatch caused loadRepoMetaState() to never deserialize bounty
reserve data, making BountyReserveOrderID always empty and finalization
always fail with 'reserve bounty belum aktif'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: filter stddev/cv aggregates from benchmark CI to prevent false positives

The _stddev and _cv statistical aggregates cause false positive regression
alerts on shared CI runners — their ratios can be 40-50x between runs due
to scheduling jitter, even when actual benchmark times are stable. Filter
them from the JSON output before comparison. Only mean/median values are
used for regression detection.

Also adds --benchmark_report_aggregates_only=true to reduce output noise.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Switch Web Push encryption from aes128gcm to aesgcm with RFC-compliant headers (#31)

* Initial plan

* fix: switch Web Push encryption to aesgcm with RFC-compliant headers

Replace the RFC 8291 aes128gcm binary-body approach with the
draft-ietf-webpush-encryption-04 aesgcm scheme so that standard push
services (Mozilla, FCM, etc.) can decrypt notifications from the device.

Key changes:
- push_encrypt: new key derivation (auth-secret IKM, P-256 context
  string); body is now ciphertext||tag only (no 86-byte binary header);
  two new output params return the per-message salt and sender key.
- push_start_delivery_for_sub: adds Encryption: salt=<base64url> and
  Crypto-Key: dh=<base64url> headers; Content-Encoding changed from
  aes128gcm to aesgcm; Authorization keeps RFC 8292 vapid t=…,k=… form.
- PUSH_ENCRYPTED_MAX_LEN updated (2+payload+16, was 86+payload+17).
- HTTP request buffer enlarged from 704 to 1024 bytes.
- Remove unused PUSH_RS_FIELD constant from push_manager.h.

Co-authored-by: phieri <12006381+phieri@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Modularise release.yml — extract each build target into its own reusable workflow (#60)

* Initial plan

* feat: modularise release.yml into per-target reusable workflows

Extract each build target from release.yml into its own reusable
workflow file so each can be triggered independently or as part of
the full orchestrated release:

- .github/workflows/release-linux.yml  (workflow_call + workflow_dispatch)
- .github/workflows/release-windows.yml (workflow_call + workflow_dispatch, tag_name input)
- .github/workflows/release-mac.yml    (workflow_call + workflow_dispatch)
- .github/workflows/release-wasm.yml   (workflow_call + workflow_dispatch)

release.yml becomes a thin orchestrator (163 lines vs 583) that calls
the four reusable workflows then runs the unchanged publish and
verify_assets jobs.

No functional changes: all steps, logic, conditions and artifact names
are identical to the original. The only logic adjustment is in the
msi_version step (release-windows.yml) which now reads from
inputs.tag_name when provided instead of github.event_name, correctly
preserving version derivation under both workflow_call and direct
workflow_dispatch triggers.

Add permissions: contents: read to every build job (was missing in the
original) to satisfy the CodeQL missing-workflow-permissions rule.

Co-authored-by: MaximumTrainer <1376575+MaximumTrainer@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: set permissions: contents: read on all caller jobs in release.yml

Replaced permissions: {} with permissions: contents: read on each of
the four build_* jobs that delegate to reusable workflows. The empty
permissions block stripped all GITHUB_TOKEN scopes, preventing
actions/checkout and artifact uploads inside the called workflows.

feat: self-healing skills + domain auto-learning + conditional branching + parallel execution

Self-healing skill system (agent_skills.rs):
- SkillStore with JSON persistence and confidence-based matching

Domain strategy auto-learning (agent_auto.rs):
- observe_page() now detects login forms, popups/modals, and CAPTCHA
- Auto-creates DomainStrategy on first encounter (popup dismiss selectors,
  anti-bot level detection)

Conditional branching (task_planner.rs + agent_auto.rs):
- PlannedAction::Conditional now generates real steps (was no-op)
- Planner injects popup dismissal conditionals when has_popup detected
- Planner injects login-wall detection when has_login_form detected
- execute_step() evaluates JS condition and reports branch taken

Parallel action execution (agent_auto.rs):
- Detects adjacent read-only steps (snapshot, screenshot, extract)
- Executes them concurrently via futures::join_all
- is_read_only_action() classifier for safe parallelization

* feat: add orphan cleanup and service-layer tests for direct transfer

Add orphaned object cleanup when finalization fails integrity checks (size/content-type mismatch). Adds 5 new service-layer tests covering unsupported storage, replay prevention, missing objects, and presigned download flows.

* fix: address PR review comments

- Add noopener/noreferrer to window.open calls to prevent tabnabbing
- Use alg-based JWT signing method validation instead of pointer comparison
- Capture single time.Now() in FinalizeDirectUpload for consistent expiry checks
- Propagate SDK SignedHeader from presign results in both PresignPut and PresignGet

* fix: open tabs synchronously in downloadAll to avoid popup blockers

Replace async sequential downloadFile() calls with synchronous
window.open() during the click handler, then resolve URLs asynchronously.
Browsers block window.open() calls that aren't in the original user
gesture's microtask, so the previous await-in-loop pattern would have
tabs blocked after the first one.

* fix: prevent duplicate uploads when direct transfer fails mid-batch

Probe direct transfer support with the first file's initiate call before
committing to a strategy. If initiate returns 409, fall back to multipart
for the entire batch. This prevents the previous bug where files already
uploaded via direct transfer would be re-uploaded via multipart when a
later file in the batch triggered a 409 fallback.

* fix: fix TypeScript type error in files test map callback

commit.message:
feat(intelligence): ARM SVE/SVE2 dot product acceleration

- DotProductSve: scalable-width LUT decode with Sve.MultiplyAdd (fused MLA)
  and Sve.AddAcross (SADDV horizontal reduction). Adapts to hardware SVE
  vector length (128-2048 bits). Zero-skip with JIT-constant-folded
  bytesPerVec branches.

- DotProductSve2: in-register ternary decode — no LUT memory access.
  Uses GatherVectorByteZeroExtend for byte replication across lanes,
  per-lane ShiftRightLogical for 2-bit field extraction, branchless
  decode via (magnitude) * (1 - signBit). Purely compute-bound path
  that eliminates LUT cache dependency.

- Dispatch order: SVE2 > SVE > AVX-512 > AVX2 > NEON > Sparse > Scalar
  (JIT dead-code-eliminates unreachable architecture branches)

- Skip index conditioning updated to exclude SVE platforms
- SYSLIB5003 suppressed (SVE/SVE2 APIs are experimental in .NET 10)
- Docs updated with full 7-tier dispatch table and SVE details
- 125 tests passing, CLI verified

commit.message:
Fix buffer index panic on display sleep/wake

Move editor.render() inside terminal.draw() closure so the
RenderFrame is computed with the actual buffer dimensions, not
a potentially stale terminal.size(). On display sleep/wake, the
terminal can resize between size() and draw(), causing pane rects
to exceed the buffer — panicking at index_of().

Also fix two related boundary issues:
- wizard render_line: remove +1 off-by-one in width, clamp to max_w
- picker query cursor: clamp to content_area.right() - 1

commit.message:
fix: maze not rendering due to canvas sized to 0 when hidden

resizeCanvas() was called in the constructor while #game-screen had
display:none, causing getBoundingClientRect() to return 0x0 and
overwrite canvas.style.width/height with '0px', defeating CSS width:100%.

- Skip resizeCanvas() when dimensions are zero
- Call resizeCanvas() at the start of startGameLoop(), by which point
  setState(PLAYING) has already made the canvas visible

commit.message:
fix: address quality audit findings (4 critical, 10 high, 6 medium)

Critical fixes:
- C2: Log warning when template variables are missing instead of silent erasure
- C3: Add recursion depth limit (32 levels) to condition parser
- C4: Check git fetch/add exit status in sync_upstream
- (C1 was fixed in prior commit: UTF-8 safe string truncation)

High fixes:
- H1: Use render_shell() for hook commands to prevent shell injection
- H2: Replace unwrap() with flatten() in parallel group results
- H3: Return ConditionError instead of silent zero for int()/float() parse failures
- H4: Add MAX_CONDITION_LEN (8192) limit to prevent DoS
- H5: Use stored working_dir as default in execute_bash_step
- H6: Pass system_prompt to claude CLI via --system-prompt flag
- H7: Propagate serde serialization errors instead of writing empty manifest
- H8: Unify DEFAULT_MAX_DEPTH to single constant in models.rs (was 3 vs 6)
- H9: Log warning on file_hash read errors instead of silent empty string

Medium fixes:
- M2: Log warnings on audit log file creation failures
- M3: Move with_depth behind #[cfg(test)]
- M4: Remove unused _depth parameter from execute_with_depth
- M5: Update docs: parallel_group is implemented, not "Future"
- M8: Make DiscoveryCache fields private, add len()/is_empty()
- M9: Move dunder check from raw condition string to identifier resolution
